reasoning: Given that only the `submit` tool is available, I am basing this forecast on the background provided, historical knowledge of BIS actions, and the crowd prediction of 28% from late October. The US implemented a significant package of export controls in January 2025, so another major expansion of AI chip, model weight, or cloud computing export controls becoming effective in the same calendar year (by December 31, 2025) would break the usual 12-to-15 month cadence we have seen (e.g., Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Jan 2025). The crowd is at 28%, meaning the event has not yet objectively happened as of October 30, and is considered less likely than not, though the non-zero chance likely reflects ongoing discussions around cloud computing controls or potential sudden Interim Final Rules. I adjust slightly downward from 0.28 to 0.26 to account for the passage of time from October 30 to November 9 without the event occurring, which continuously shortens the window for a rule to be announced and enacted before the end of the year.
